subject: URGENT: Your SBI account has been suspended - Immediate action required
body:
Dear Customer,

Your State Bank of India account ending in XXXX has been SUSPENDED due to suspicious activity detected on your account. Your account will be permanently closed within 24 HOURS if you do not verify your details immediately.

Click the link below to verify your KYC and restore access:
hxxp://sbi-secure-kyc[.]in/verify-now

You will need to provide:
- Aadhaar number
- PAN Card number
- Internet banking password
- OTP sent to registered mobile

Failure to comply will result in permanent account closure and legal proceedings.

SBI Customer Care
1800-XXX-XXXX
label: phishing
scenario: SBI phishing email threatening account suspension, demanding KYC and credentials
explanation: This email is labeled phishing due to multiple red flags: the sender domain (sbi-secure-kyc.in) does not match SBI's official domain (sbi.co.in), and the email requests highly sensitive credentials including Aadhaar, PAN, and banking password in combination. Real banks never ask for passwords or full credential sets via email. The threat of 'legal proceedings' and '24 hour' deadline are classic urgency/fear tactics. The CAPS usage and generic 'Dear Customer' greeting are additional indicators.

subject: Your HDFC Bank Account Statement for March 2025 is Ready
body:
Dear Rahul Sharma,

Your HDFC Bank account statement for the period 01-Mar-2025 to 31-Mar-2025 is now available.

Account Number: XXXX XXXX 4821
Statement Period: 01 March 2025 - 31 March 2025
Closing Balance: INR 47,234.50

You can view and download your statement by logging into NetBanking at hxxps://netbanking[.]hdfcbank[.]com or through the HDFC Bank Mobile App.

If you have not received the statement or have any queries, please call our 24x7 Customer Care at 1800-202-6161 (toll-free).

Please do not reply to this email. This is an auto-generated message.

Warm regards,
HDFC Bank Customer Service

HDFC Bank Ltd., HDFC Bank House, Senapati Bapat Marg, Lower Parel, Mumbai - 400 013.

This email is intended solely for the addressee. If you are not the intended recipient, please notify the sender.

[Unsubscribe] [Privacy Policy]
label: genuine
scenario: Genuine HDFC Bank monthly account statement notification email
explanation: This email is genuine because the sender domain (hdfcbank.com) matches the official HDFC Bank domain, the recipient is addressed by name (personalized greeting), and the email includes HDFC Bank's registered physical address. It directs users only to the official netbanking.hdfcbank.com domain and does not request any credentials. The presence of an unsubscribe link and privacy policy are hallmarks of legitimate institutional communication.

subject: Congratulations! You have won Rs.50,000 HDFC Reward Points - Redeem Now
body:
DEAR VALUED CUSTOMER!!!

CONGRATULATIONS!!! You have been SELECTED as the WINNER of HDFC Bank's 25th Anniversary Loyalty Reward Program!!!

You have WON 50,000 Reward Points (Worth Rs.50,000 CASH)!!!

To CLAIM your reward you must verify your identity within 48 HOURS or the reward will be FORFEITED!!!

STEP 1: Click here to verify: hxxp://hdfc-rewards-claim[.]xyz/redeem
STEP 2: Enter your Debit Card number and CVV
STEP 3: Enter OTP sent to your mobile
STEP 4: Your reward will be credited instantly!!!

This offer is EXCLUSIVE and NON-TRANSFERABLE. Only valid for account holders.

HDFC Rewards Team
noreply@hdfc-rewards-claim[.]xyz
label: phishing
scenario: HDFC Bank prize/reward phishing email demanding debit card details and OTP
explanation: Clearly phishing: the sender domain (.xyz TLD) has no relation to HDFC Bank's official domain, and the email requests debit card number plus CVV — information no legitimate bank rewards program would ever solicit via email. Excessive capitalization, multiple exclamation marks, and the 48-hour forfeiture threat are hallmark high-noise phishing patterns. The 'lottery-style' prize framing (you were 'selected') with no prior context is a textbook prize notification scam.

subject: OTP for ICICI Bank NetBanking Login - Do Not Share
body:
Dear Customer,

Your One Time Password (OTP) for ICICI Bank NetBanking login is:

847291

This OTP is valid for 10 minutes only. Do NOT share this OTP with anyone, including ICICI Bank employees.

If you have not initiated this login, please call our 24x7 helpline at 1800-1080 immediately and change your NetBanking password.

Ref No: ICICI/NB/2025/0319/A4729

Regards,
ICICI Bank

This is a system-generated message. Please do not reply to this email.
label: genuine
scenario: Genuine ICICI Bank OTP email for NetBanking login - may appear suspicious due to containing an OTP
explanation: This is a genuine OTP email from ICICI Bank sent from alerts@icicibank.com, an official domain. It contains a reference number, no external URLs, and explicitly warns users not to share the OTP — a hallmark of legitimate bank communications. However, a naive classifier might flag this as phishing because it contains a credential (OTP) and warns of suspicious activity. The key distinguishing signals are the official sender domain, the absence of any clickable links, and the defensive 'do not share' instruction.

subject: Action Required: Complete Your KYC Before 31st March or Account Will Be Blocked
body:
Dear SBI Account Holder,

As per RBI guidelines, all customers must complete their KYC (Know Your Customer) verification by 31st March 2025. Failure to do so will result in your account being temporarily blocked.

We noticed that your KYC documents are pending update. Please complete the process within 7 days to avoid any disruption to your banking services.

Complete KYC Online: hxxp://sbi-kyc-update[.]co[.]in/complete

Documents Required:
- Aadhaar Card (front and back)
- PAN Card
- Recent Photograph
- Bank Account Number and IFSC

For assistance, contact: support@sbi-kyc-update[.]co[.]in

State Bank of India
Customer Service Division
label: phishing
sender_display: State Bank of India - KYC Team
scenario: SBI KYC phishing email invoking RBI authority to harvest Aadhaar and PAN documents
explanation: This is phishing despite its professional tone and low noise level. The sender domain (sbi-kyc-update.co.in) is not SBI's official domain (sbi.co.in), and the URL follows the same pattern. The email invokes RBI authority to add legitimacy — a common authority impersonation technique. Requesting Aadhaar and PAN documents via an external link is a red flag; SBI's legitimate KYC processes occur through verified in-branch or official app channels. The medium false positive risk arises because KYC deadline emails are genuinely sent by banks periodically.

subject: PayTm: Your transaction of Rs.2,499 to Flipkart is Successful
body:
Hi Priya,

Your payment was successful!

Transaction Details:
Amount: Rs. 2,499.00
Paid to: Flipkart Internet Pvt Ltd
UPI ID: flipkart@ybl
Date & Time: 18 Mar 2025, 03:42 PM
Transaction ID: PTM2025031847291034
UTR Number: 317284910293

Your Paytm Wallet balance: Rs. 342.50

If you did not make this transaction, please raise a dispute within the Paytm app under Help & Support > Transaction Issues.

Download Paytm: hxxps://paytm[.]com/download

Thanks for using Paytm!
Team Paytm

© 2025 One97 Communications Limited. All rights reserved.
601, 6th Floor, Copia Corporate Suites, Jasola District Centre, New Delhi - 110025.
label: genuine
scenario: Genuine Paytm UPI payment success notification with full transaction details
explanation: This is a genuine transaction alert from Paytm. The sender email matches Paytm's official domain (paytm.com), the recipient is addressed by first name, and it contains a transaction ID and UTR number — both verifiable reference points. The physical address of One97 Communications (Paytm's parent) is included and accurate. The only URL present is paytm.com itself. Legitimate Paytm alerts always include a UTR number and advise disputing via the app, not via email links.

subject: Your UPI payment of Rs.15,000 has FAILED - Refund Pending - Verify Now
body:
Dear Customer,

We regret to inform you that your UPI payment of Rs.15,000 has FAILED due to a technical error on our servers.

Transaction ID: UPI20250318XXXXXXXX
Status: FAILED
Refund Status: PENDING

To initiate your refund immediately, please verify your UPI PIN and bank account details at:
hxxp://upi-refund-portal[.]in/claim-refund

Please complete verification within 2 hours. After this window, the refund process will be delayed by 15-30 business days.

NPCI Refund Helpdesk
noreply@upi-refund-portal[.]in
label: phishing
scenario: NPCI/UPI impersonation phishing claiming failed transaction refund to harvest UPI PIN
explanation: This is a phishing email impersonating NPCI, the body governing UPI in India. The sender domain (upi-refund-portal.in) is not related to NPCI's official domain (npci.org.in). NPCI and banks never send refund claim links via email requiring UPI PINs — refunds for failed transactions are processed automatically. The 2-hour urgency window is a pressure tactic. This email has medium false positive risk because failed UPI transactions are common and users who've recently had failures may find the premise plausible.

subject: ICICI Bank: Credit Card Bill Payment Reminder - Due Date: 25 March 2025
body:
Dear Amit Verma,

This is a friendly reminder that your ICICI Bank Credit Card payment is due.

Card Number: XXXX XXXX XXXX 7834
Total Amount Due: Rs. 23,481.00
Minimum Amount Due: Rs. 1,174.00
Payment Due Date: 25 March 2025

To avoid late payment charges and interest, please make your payment by the due date.

Pay Now via:
- ICICI Bank NetBanking: hxxps://netbanking[.]icicibank[.]com
- ICICI Bank iMobile Pay App
- NEFT/RTGS to ICICI Bank
- Auto-debit (if registered)

For queries, call: 1800-1080 (24x7, toll-free)

Warm regards,
ICICI Bank Credit Cards

ICICI Bank Limited, ICICI Bank Towers, Bandra-Kurla Complex, Mumbai 400 051.
[Unsubscribe from Marketing Emails] | [Privacy Policy]
label: genuine
scenario: Genuine ICICI Bank credit card bill payment reminder with due date
explanation: Genuine email from ICICI Bank's official domain (icicibank.com). The recipient is addressed by full name with masked card number, and all payment options reference official ICICI channels (NetBanking, iMobile Pay). The physical address of ICICI Bank's HQ in BKC Mumbai is accurate. Multiple payment methods are offered with no urgency pressure beyond the due date. Presence of unsubscribe link and privacy policy confirm this is legitimate institutional communication.

subject: RBI Notice: Your Bank Account Linked to Money Laundering Activity
body:
NOTICE FROM RESERVE BANK OF INDIA

Dear Account Holder,

This is an OFFICIAL NOTICE from the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Fraud Prevention Cell.

Your bank account has been flagged for SUSPICIOUS TRANSACTIONS linked to money laundering activity under Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA) 2002.

Your account will be SEIZED within 24 hours unless you respond to this notice.

To dispute this action and verify your identity:
hxxp://rbi-fraud-cell[.]gov[.]in[.]verify-account[.]com/dispute

Contact our Cyber Fraud Officer immediately:
Email: rbi.fraudcell@rbi-gov[.]in
Phone: +91-9XXXXXXXXX

Reserve Bank of India
Fraud Prevention Cell, New Delhi
label: phishing
sender_display: Reserve Bank of India - Fraud Cell
scenario: RBI impersonation phishing email threatening money laundering charges to induce panic
explanation: Classic government authority impersonation phishing. RBI's official domain is rbi.org.in — not rbi-gov.in. The URL is an elaborate domain spoofing attempt embedding 'gov.in' as a subdomain component while the actual registrable domain is verify-account.com. RBI does not communicate with individuals via email regarding money laundering investigations — such matters go through legal/judicial channels. The '24-hour seizure' threat is designed to override rational thinking with panic. No legitimate government body provides mobile numbers for such investigations.

subject: SBI: Your Fixed Deposit of Rs.1,00,000 has Matured
body:
Dear Sunita Agarwal,

We are pleased to inform you that your Fixed Deposit has matured.

FD Details:
Account Number: XXXX4921
FD Reference Number: SBI/FD/2025/0318/94821
Principal Amount: Rs. 1,00,000.00
Maturity Amount: Rs. 1,07,421.00
Maturity Date: 18 March 2025
Interest Earned: Rs. 7,421.00

As per your instructions, the maturity amount of Rs. 1,07,421.00 has been credited to your Savings Account XXXX4921 today.

To manage your deposits or renew your FD, please log in to YONO SBI: hxxps://yono[.]sbi[.]co[.]in

For any queries, please visit your nearest SBI branch or call 1800-11-2211.

Thank you for banking with us.

State Bank of India
Customer Service

Corporate Centre, State Bank Bhavan, Madame Cama Road, Mumbai - 400 021
[Privacy Policy] | [Unsubscribe]
label: genuine
scenario: Genuine SBI Fixed Deposit maturity notification with credit confirmation
explanation: This is a genuine SBI Fixed Deposit maturity notification. The sender domain (sbi.co.in) is SBI's official domain, the recipient is addressed by name with masked account number, and the FD reference number is provided. The email confirms a credit already made (not directing to a link to 'claim' money), and points only to the official YONO SBI platform. SBI's registered corporate address in Mumbai is accurate. All signals align with standard legitimate bank communication.

subject: Your SBI Net Banking Password Expires Tomorrow - Reset Immediately
body:
Dear SBI NetBanking User,

This is an automated security notification from State Bank of India.

Your Internet Banking password is scheduled to expire on 19 March 2025 (Tomorrow). As per RBI's digital security mandate, passwords older than 90 days must be reset.

To reset your password and continue uninterrupted access:
hxxps://sbi-netbanking-secure[.]com/password-reset

Please keep the following ready:
- Customer ID
- Current Password
- New Password
- OTP (will be sent to registered mobile)

If you do not reset within 24 hours, your NetBanking access will be suspended for 30 days as a security measure.

Regards,
SBI Security Team
alerts@sbi-netbanking-secure[.]com
label: phishing
scenario: SBI phishing email impersonating a password expiry notice to harvest NetBanking credentials
explanation: This is phishing despite its professional tone. The sender domain (sbi-netbanking-secure.com) is not sbi.co.in. Password reset flows for legitimate banks never require submitting the current password via email. The invocation of an 'RBI mandate' is fabricated to add authority. This email has medium false positive risk because password expiry notifications are genuinely sent by banks and IT systems, and the low noise level could fool users who don't inspect sender domains carefully.

subject: HDFC Bank: Rs.3,200 Cashback Credited to Your Account
body:
Dear Rohit Mehta,

Great news! A cashback of Rs. 3,200.00 has been credited to your HDFC Bank Savings Account.

Cashback Details:
Amount Credited: Rs. 3,200.00
Credit Date: 15 March 2025
Reason: Festive Season Offer - Online Shopping Cashback (Q4 2024-25)
Account Credited: XXXX7213
Reference ID: HDFC/CB/2025/0315/72841

Your current account balance will reflect this credit. Please allow 2-4 hours for the amount to show in your statement.

This cashback was applied per your opted-in offer registered on 12 Jan 2025.

For any queries regarding this cashback, please contact HDFC Bank Customer Care at 1800-202-6161 or visit your nearest HDFC Bank branch.

Warm regards,
HDFC Bank Rewards & Offers Team

HDFC Bank Ltd., HDFC Bank House, Senapati Bapat Marg, Lower Parel, Mumbai - 400 013.
[Unsubscribe] [Privacy Policy]
label: genuine
scenario: Genuine HDFC Bank cashback credit notification - could look like reward bait to a classifier
explanation: This is a genuine cashback notification from HDFC Bank's official domain. The recipient is addressed by name, the account is partially masked, a reference ID is provided, and the cashback is tied to a previously registered offer (prior relationship mentioned). Notably, no external URLs are present — the email only advises calling HDFC's toll-free number or visiting a branch. The high false positive risk stems from the fact that 'cashback credited' language combined with a specific amount can superficially resemble reward-bait phishing to a naive classifier.

subject: Immediate Action Required: Suspicious Login Detected on Your ICICI Account
body:
Dear ICICI Bank Customer,

We have detected a suspicious login attempt on your ICICI Bank NetBanking account from an unrecognized device:

Device: Unknown Android Device
Location: Jaipur, Rajasthan
Time: 18 March 2025, 11:47 PM IST
IP Address: 103.XX.XX.47

If this was NOT you, click below to secure your account immediately:
hxxp://icici-securelogin[.]net/block-device

If this WAS you, click here to confirm:
hxxp://icici-securelogin[.]net/confirm

Both actions require your NetBanking password and OTP verification.

Do NOT ignore this alert. Unverified access may result in account lock.

ICICI Bank Security Team
security@icici-securelogin[.]net
label: phishing
scenario: ICICI Bank account security alert phishing email reporting fake suspicious login
explanation: This is phishing despite its realistic security-alert format. The sender domain (icici-securelogin.net) is not icicibank.com. A particularly clever tactic here is offering two links — one to 'block' and one to 'confirm' — both leading to the same malicious domain and both requiring credentials. Genuine bank security alerts either block suspicious sessions automatically or ask users to call a toll-free number, not to re-enter passwords via email links. The high false positive risk is significant because suspicious login alerts from real banks look nearly identical to this format.

subject: PayTm Postpaid: Your November Bill of Rs.4,821 is Due
body:
Hi Deepak,

Your Paytm Postpaid bill for November is ready.

Bill Summary:
Bill Period: 01 Nov 2024 - 30 Nov 2024
Total Amount Due: Rs. 4,821.00
Payment Due Date: 10 December 2024
Bill Number: PTPB/2024/11/DK8472

Pay before 10th December to avoid late charges.

Pay Now via Paytm App → Postpaid → Pay Bill

Or visit: hxxps://paytm[.]com/postpaid

For help: hxxps://paytm[.]com/care

Thanks,
Paytm Postpaid Team

© 2024 One97 Communications Ltd.
601, 6th Floor, Copia Corporate Suites, Jasola District Centre, New Delhi - 110025.
label: genuine
scenario: Genuine Paytm Postpaid monthly bill notification with app-based payment instructions
explanation: Genuine Paytm Postpaid bill notification from official domain paytm.com. User is addressed by first name, a bill number is provided, and all URLs reference paytm.com exclusively. The email primarily directs users to pay through the Paytm app rather than via a web link, which is the standard Paytm Postpaid UX. The registered physical address of One97 Communications matches public records. No credentials are requested and no pressure tactics are employed.

subject: Your SBI Account Will Be Permanently Deactivated in 48 Hours
body:
URGENT NOTICE

Dear SBI Customer,

Your State Bank of India account associated with this email address is scheduled for PERMANENT DEACTIVATION in 48 hours.

Reason: Non-compliance with updated RBI Digital Banking norms (Circular DBS.CO.RCBD/BC.10/13.02.001/2024-25)

To prevent deactivation, please update your account by clicking below:
hxxp://sbi-digital-compliance[.]org/update

You will need to confirm:
1. Account number and IFSC
2. Registered mobile number
3. Aadhaar-linked OTP

This is your FINAL notice. No further reminders will be sent.

State Bank of India
Digital Compliance Team
RBI Reference: DBS2024-25-10-XXXX
label: phishing
scenario: SBI phishing email citing fake RBI circular to harvest account and Aadhaar-linked OTP
explanation: Phishing email that cites a specific-looking but fabricated RBI circular number to appear official. The sender domain (.org) is inconsistent with SBI's .co.in domain. Real RBI circulars are publicly available on rbi.org.in and banks communicate compliance requirements through in-app notifications and branch communications, not email deactivation threats. The use of 'FINAL notice' and 'PERMANENT DEACTIVATION' are fear amplifiers. The medium false positive risk is because RBI genuinely issues periodic compliance circulars that banks do communicate about.

subject: SBI YONO: Your Loan EMI of Rs.12,450 has been auto-debited
body:
Dear Manish Kumar,

Your SBI Home Loan EMI has been successfully processed.

EMI Debit Details:
Loan Account: SBIHL/2021/MU/48291
EMI Amount: Rs. 12,450.00
Debit Date: 15 March 2025
From Account: XXXX8821
Remaining Principal: Rs. 18,42,310.00
Next EMI Date: 15 April 2025

Transaction Reference: YONO/EMI/2025/0315/88291

You can view your loan statement and schedule on YONO SBI: hxxps://yono[.]sbi[.]co[.]in

For queries, call SBI Home Loans: 1800-11-2018 (toll-free, 24x7)

Thank you for your continued trust in State Bank of India.

State Bank of India - Home Loans
Corporate Centre, State Bank Bhavan, Madame Cama Road, Mumbai - 400 021.
[Privacy Policy] [Unsubscribe]
label: genuine
scenario: Genuine SBI YONO Home Loan EMI auto-debit confirmation notification
explanation: Genuine SBI loan EMI debit notification from homeloans@sbi.co.in, an official SBI domain. The email contains a loan account number, remaining principal balance, and a transaction reference — all details a legitimate EMI confirmation would include. The only URL points to YONO SBI's official domain (yono.sbi.co.in). No credentials are requested, and the tone is informational rather than urgent. Physical address and unsubscribe link confirm institutional legitimacy.

subject: ICICI Bank: Your Debit Card ending 7812 used for Rs.89,999 at Amazon - Confirm?
body:
Dear Customer,

A transaction has been attempted on your ICICI Bank Debit Card.

Transaction Details:
Card: XXXX XXXX XXXX 7812
Amount: Rs. 89,999.00
Merchant: Amazon India
Date: 18 March 2025, 08:12 PM IST

Did YOU make this purchase?

YES - Confirm Transaction: hxxp://icici-txn-confirm[.]in/yes
NO - Block Card & Dispute: hxxp://icici-txn-confirm[.]in/no

Both options require your NetBanking credentials to proceed.

ICICI Bank Fraud Prevention
security@icici-txn-confirm[.]in
label: phishing
scenario: ICICI Bank transaction confirmation phishing posing as a fraud alert for a large purchase
explanation: Phishing using a highly effective tactic: a large transaction amount (Rs.89,999) designed to trigger panic, with both YES and NO options requiring NetBanking credentials. Both links resolve to the same malicious domain (icici-txn-confirm.in). Genuine ICICI Bank transaction alerts sent via SMS/email for potential fraud never request credentials to confirm or deny — they use one-tap SMS responses or in-app verification. This email has very high false positive risk because the format closely mirrors real ICICI fraud alert emails; only the sender domain and the credential requirement betray it.

subject: HDFC Bank NetBanking: You have 3 new eStatements available
body:
Dear Kavitha Reddy,

Your HDFC Bank eStatements are now available for download.

Available Statements:
1. Savings Account XXXX2910 - February 2025
2. Savings Account XXXX2910 - January 2025
3. Credit Card XXXX5821 - February 2025

To access your statements:
1. Log in to HDFC Bank NetBanking: hxxps://netbanking[.]hdfcbank[.]com
2. Go to Accounts > eStatements
3. Select the statement period

Statements are available in PDF format. You may need Adobe Acrobat Reader to view them.

Note: eStatements are your official bank records and can be used for tax filing purposes.

For assistance, please call 1800-202-6161 (24x7, toll-free).

Warm regards,
HDFC Bank NetBanking Team

HDFC Bank Ltd., HDFC Bank House, Senapati Bapat Marg, Lower Parel, Mumbai - 400 013.
[Unsubscribe] [Privacy Policy]
label: genuine
scenario: Genuine HDFC Bank eStatement availability notification directing to official NetBanking portal
explanation: Genuine HDFC Bank eStatement notification from netbanking@hdfcbank.com, an official HDFC domain. The email addresses the user by name with partially masked account numbers and instructs users to log in to the official NetBanking portal to download statements — not via an email attachment. Instructions are clear and step-by-step, consistent with HDFC's customer communications. Physical address, unsubscribe link, and privacy policy all confirm legitimacy.

subject: Alert: Your PayTm KYC is Incomplete - UPI Payments Will Stop in 24 Hours
body:
Dear Paytm User,

Important Notice regarding your Paytm account.

Our records show your Paytm KYC is INCOMPLETE. As per SEBI and RBI regulations, incomplete KYC accounts will have UPI payment functionality DISABLED after 24 hours.

To complete your KYC instantly:
hxxp://paytm-kyc-verify[.]com/complete-kyc

You will need:
- Aadhaar Number (12 digits)
- PAN Card Number
- Selfie for face match
- Bank account linked to Paytm

KYC takes only 2 minutes. Complete now to avoid payment disruption.

Paytm KYC Team
kyc@paytm-kyc-verify[.]com

This notification was sent as your Paytm account is registered with this email.
label: phishing
scenario: Paytm KYC phishing email threatening UPI disablement to harvest Aadhaar and PAN
explanation: Phishing impersonating Paytm's KYC process. The sender domain (paytm-kyc-verify.com) is not paytm.com. Paytm's legitimate KYC is completed entirely within the Paytm app using the app's built-in camera and verification flow — not via external links. The claim of SEBI involvement (SEBI regulates securities, not UPI) is a regulatory inaccuracy designed to sound authoritative. Medium false positive risk because Paytm genuinely does send KYC completion reminders and has done so historically when RBI imposed wallet KYC deadlines.

subject: HDFC Bank: Your UPI AutoPay mandate registered for Rs.999/month
body:
Dear Pooja Nair,

A UPI AutoPay mandate has been successfully registered on your HDFC Bank account.

Mandate Details:
Mandate ID: HDFC/AUTOPAY/2025/0317/MP9821
Biller: Netflix India
Amount: Rs. 999.00 per month
Start Date: 17 March 2025
Frequency: Monthly
Debit Account: XXXX6291
UPI ID: pooja.nair@hdfcbank

The first debit will occur on 17 April 2025.

If you did NOT register this mandate, please:
1. Call HDFC Bank immediately: 1800-202-6161
2. Or manage mandates via HDFC Bank UPI app

Important: HDFC Bank will never ask you for your UPI PIN or password to cancel a mandate.

Regards,
HDFC Bank UPI Services

HDFC Bank Ltd., Lower Parel, Mumbai - 400 013.
[Unsubscribe] [Privacy Policy]
label: phishing
scenario: HDFC Bank UPI AutoPay mandate phishing email that closely mimics legitimate mandate alerts
explanation: This is phishing despite closely mimicking a genuine HDFC UPI AutoPay mandate notification. The sender domain (hdfc-autopay-secure.net) is not hdfcbank.com. The email is designed to alarm the recipient about an unauthorized Netflix AutoPay mandate to pressure them into clicking the included link to "cancel" it, where credentials will be harvested. This sample has high false positive risk because legitimate HDFC mandate notifications use an almost identical format and language, making it exceptionally difficult for both automated classifiers and users to distinguish without inspecting the sender domain.

subject: Your SBI account has been compromised. Verify identity to restore access.
body:
Dear Valued Customer,

We are writing to inform you that our security systems have flagged unusual activity on your State Bank of India account. To protect your financial assets, we have placed a temporary hold on your account pending identity verification.

To restore full access to your account, please complete the verification process at your earliest convenience:

hxxps://secure-sbi-portal[.]com/identity-verify

During the verification process, you will be required to confirm your account credentials, registered contact information, and answer a few security questions. The process takes approximately three minutes.

If you believe this hold was placed in error or have not noticed any unusual account activity, please still complete the verification to confirm your identity. Failure to complete verification within 72 hours will result in the temporary hold becoming permanent.

We apologize for the inconvenience and thank you for your cooperation.

Sincerely,
SBI Security Operations Center
label: phishing
scenario: AI-generated SBI account compromise phishing email with professional tone and no typos
explanation: This is an AI-generated phishing email identifiable by its clean, formal language, balanced sentence structure, and complete absence of typographical errors — hallmarks of LLM-generated text. The sender domain (secure-sbi-portal.com) is not sbi.co.in. A distinctive AI-generated manipulation appears in 'If you believe this was an error, please still complete verification' — forcing both 'yes' and 'no' responses to lead to credential harvesting. The polished professionalism of this email makes it more dangerous than high-noise phishing as it may pass naive spam filters.

subject: Important Notice: Immediate KYC Update Required for Your HDFC Bank Account
body:
Dear Account Holder,

We are reaching out to inform you that as part of our ongoing compliance with Reserve Bank of India directives, all HDFC Bank customers are required to update their KYC documentation.

Our records indicate that your account's KYC information requires renewal. Accounts with expired or incomplete KYC documentation are subject to operational restrictions, including limitations on transactions and fund transfers, in accordance with the Prevention of Money Laundering Act.

To complete your KYC update, please visit the following secure link and follow the on-screen instructions:

hxxps://hdfc-kyc-portal[.]net/update

You will need your Aadhaar number, PAN card, and a recent utility bill or bank statement as proof of address. The process is straightforward and takes approximately five minutes to complete.

We appreciate your prompt attention to this matter and your continued trust in HDFC Bank.

Kind regards,
Compliance and Customer Services Division
HDFC Bank Limited
label: phishing
scenario: AI-generated HDFC Bank KYC compliance phishing with perfect grammar and formal language
explanation: Second AI-generated phishing sample, identifiable by its uniformly formal register, zero typos, and LLM-characteristic hedging phrases ('straightforward and takes approximately five minutes'). The domain hdfc-kyc-portal.net is not hdfcbank.com. Real HDFC KYC updates are conducted through the HDFC Bank app, NetBanking portal, or physical branches — never via external .net domains. The email notably avoids aggressive urgency language while still conveying compliance pressure, a subtle AI-generation pattern. The generic salutation 'Dear Account Holder' despite the professional tone is a tell.

subject: SBI: International transaction of $2,800 on your card - Confirm?
body:
Dear SBI Cardholder,

An international transaction has been attempted on your SBI Debit/Credit Card:

Merchant: Amazon USA
Amount: USD 2,800.00 (approx INR 2,33,184)
Location: Seattle, WA, USA
Date: 19 March 2025, 02:18 AM IST

If this was YOU: No action required.

If this was NOT YOU: Call SBI Card immediately at 1800-180-1290 to block your card. Do NOT click any link.

For 24x7 card blocking: 1800-180-1290 (toll-free)
Alternately, block via YONO SBI app.

SBI Cards & Payment Services Ltd.
alerts@sbicard.co.in
label: genuine
scenario: Genuine SBI Card international transaction alert - high false positive risk due to large international amount
explanation: This is a genuine SBI Card international transaction alert. The sender domain (sbicard.co.in) is SBI Card's legitimate domain. Critically, the email explicitly instructs users NOT to click any link and to call a toll-free number instead — the exact opposite of phishing behavior. No URLs are present whatsoever. High false positive risk arises because the email reports a large international USD transaction, which triggers pattern-matching in naive classifiers alongside keywords like 'confirm' and 'block your card.' A security analyst would immediately note the absence of any link as the definitive genuine signal.

subject: Paytm: Your Wallet is Credited with Rs.200 Cashback from Swiggy
body:
Hi Neha,

Your Paytm Wallet has been credited!

Credit Details:
Amount: Rs. 200.00
From: Swiggy Cashback Offer
Date: 17 March 2025
Transaction ID: PTM2025031700SWGY2934
Wallet Balance: Rs. 843.50

This cashback was applied on your Swiggy order #SW-MUM-2025-847291 placed on 16 March 2025.

View your wallet at hxxps://paytm[.]com/wallet

Happy eating!
Team Paytm

© 2025 One97 Communications Limited
601, 6th Floor, Copia Corporate Suites, New Delhi - 110025.
label: genuine
scenario: Genuine Paytm cashback credit notification linked to a specific prior order
explanation: Genuine Paytm wallet credit notification from noreply@paytm.com. The email is tied to a specific prior Swiggy order number (prior relationship mentioned), includes a transaction ID, and shows the updated wallet balance. The only URL is paytm.com directly. The casual tone ('Happy eating!') is consistent with Paytm's communication style. The connection to a real prior order makes this clearly transactional and not a generic reward-bait lure.

subject: URGENT: Your ICICI NetBanking blocked! Rs.3,42,000 debit attempted
body:
YOUR ICICI NETBANKING IS BLOCKED!!!

Dear customer An UNAUTHORISED debit of Rs3,42,000 was attmpted from ur netbanking!!! We have BLOCKED ur account temporarily.

To UNBLOCK IMMEDIATELY click:
hxxp://icici-unblock-now[.]in/restore

ENTER: Ur Customer ID + Password + OTP

If u dont verify in next 30 MINUTES ur account will be PERMANENTLY LOCKED and u may loose all funds!!!

ACT NOW!!! DONT IGNORE!!!

ICICI Fraud Team
noreply@icici-unblock-now[.]in
label: phishing
scenario: High-noise ICICI Bank phishing with SMS-style abbreviations and extreme urgency about large unauthorized debit
explanation: Clearly phishing with every high-noise hallmark: SMS-style abbreviations ('ur', 'dont', 'attmpted'), excessive capitalization, multiple exclamation marks, and an unrealistically short 30-minute ultimatum. The domain icici-unblock-now.in bears no relation to icicibank.com. The large round figure (Rs.3,42,000) is chosen to maximize panic. Despite the low sophistication, this type of phishing continues to be effective against users who primarily communicate via WhatsApp/SMS and are less attuned to email formality norms. Extremely low false positive risk.

subject: ICICI Bank: Your Salary Credit of Rs.68,500 on 15 March 2025
body:
Dear Arun Krishnamurthy,

Your account has been credited.

Transaction Details:
Credit Amount: Rs. 68,500.00
Value Date: 15 March 2025
Beneficiary Account: XXXX XXXX XXXX 3821
Transaction Type: NEFT - Salary Credit
Narration: SAL/MAR25/TECHCORP/AK
Transaction Reference: ICICIN25031588294

Available Balance: Rs. 71,842.30

This alert was sent to your registered email ID. To update your notification preferences, log in to ICICI Bank NetBanking.

ICICI Bank Ltd.
alerts@icicibank.com | 1800-1080
ICICI Bank Tower, Bandra Kurla Complex, Mumbai 400 051.
label: genuine
scenario: Genuine ICICI Bank salary credit alert with NEFT transaction details
explanation: Genuine ICICI Bank salary credit notification from alerts@icicibank.com. Contains a full transaction reference, NEFT narration string (a technical identifier used in interbank transfers), and available balance. No URLs are present — the email is purely informational with no calls to action. The narration 'SAL/MAR25/TECHCORP/AK' reflects the actual NEFT narration format used by payroll systems, adding authenticity that a phishing email would be unlikely to replicate accurately.

subject: RBI is giving FREE money!! Claim Rs.10,000 government relief now
body:
DEAR CITIZEN!!!

RESERVE BANK OF INDIA RELIEF SCHEME 2025!!!

Government of India has announced Rs10,000 direct benifit transfer for all bank account holders under PM Digital Inclusion scheme!!!

CLAIM YOUR FREE Rs.10,000 NOW:
hxxp://rbi-relief-2025[.]in/claim

Required details:
- Aadhaar Number
- Bank Account + IFSC
- UPI ID and PIN
- Mobile OTP

OFFER ENDS MIDNIGHT TONIGHT!!! Once scheme is close u cannot apply again!!! SHARE THIS WITH FAMILY!!!

PM Direct Benifit Team
RBI Authorised
rbi-scheme@rbi-relief-2025[.]in
label: phishing
scenario: Fake RBI government relief scheme phishing harvesting Aadhaar, UPI PIN, and OTP
explanation: Extremely low-sophistication phishing impersonating a government relief scheme. Multiple spelling errors ('benifit', 'authorised' inconsistency, 'close' instead of 'closed') mark this as high-noise. The request for UPI PIN directly is an immediate red flag — UPI PINs are never requested by any legitimate party. The domain rbi-relief-2025.in is unrelated to RBI's rbi.org.in. RBI does not make direct benefit transfers; that is the mandate of NPCI and DBT-linked ministries. The 'Share with family' instruction is a social engineering tactic to expand reach.

subject: HDFC Bank: NetBanking OTP for Fund Transfer - Valid 10 mins
body:
Dear Preethi Subramaniam,

Your One Time Password for HDFC Bank NetBanking fund transfer is:

592847

This OTP is valid for 10 minutes from the time of this email.

Transaction Details:
Beneficiary: Suresh Kumar
Account: XXXXXXXX8291
Amount: Rs. 25,000.00
Time Initiated: 19 March 2025, 04:23 PM IST
Ref: HDFC/NB/OTP/2025/0319/PQ7281

DO NOT share this OTP with anyone. HDFC Bank employees will never ask for your OTP.

If you did not initiate this transaction, please call 1800-202-6161 immediately.

HDFC Bank NetBanking
alerts@hdfcbank.com
label: genuine
scenario: Genuine HDFC Bank fund transfer OTP email with full transaction detail - high FP risk due to OTP presence
explanation: Genuine HDFC Bank OTP email from alerts@hdfcbank.com. The email includes the beneficiary name, partially masked account, amount, and a reference number — level of detail only the originating bank's system would possess. No URLs are present and the email explicitly warns against sharing the OTP. High false positive risk because the email contains an OTP (a credential type) and mentions a Rs.25,000 fund transfer — surface features that superficially resemble phishing transaction alerts. A trained classifier must recognize that OTP delivery emails from verified bank domains are legitimate.

subject: SBI Life Insurance: Your Premium of Rs.18,200 Auto-Debited Successfully
body:
Dear Vijay Patel,

Your SBI Life Insurance premium has been successfully collected.

Premium Collection Details:
Policy Number: 1W-2489-3821-0004
Policy Type: SBI Life Smart Wealth Builder
Premium Amount: Rs. 18,200.00
Collection Date: 18 March 2025
Next Due Date: 18 March 2026 (Annual)
Mode: Auto-Debit from HDFC Bank XXXX7821
Receipt Number: SBIL/2025/0318/VJ9821

Your policy is active and in good standing.

To view your policy details, download premium receipts, or make changes:
SBI Life Insurance website: hxxps://www[.]sbilife[.]co[.]in
SBI Life mCover App

For queries: 1800-267-9090 (toll-free, Mon-Sat 9AM-6PM)

SBI Life Insurance Co. Ltd.,
Central Processing Centre, Kapas Bhavan, Plot No. 3, Sector 10, CBD Belapur, Navi Mumbai - 400 614.
label: genuine
scenario: Genuine SBI Life Insurance annual premium auto-debit confirmation with policy details
explanation: Genuine SBI Life Insurance premium collection email from customerservice@sbilife.co.in, an official SBI Life domain. Contains a policy number, receipt number, and next due date — data points only the insurer's system would generate. The physical address (Kapas Bhavan, CBD Belapur) is SBI Life's documented CPC address. URL references only sbilife.co.in, which is the company's official domain. The debit source account (HDFC Bank XXXX7821) demonstrates cross-institution transaction recording, a hallmark of legitimate insurance premium systems.

subject: Attention: Your HDFC Debit Card Has Been Temporarily Suspended - Verify Identity
body:
Dear HDFC Bank Customer,

Due to multiple failed login attempts detected on your account, your HDFC Debit Card has been temporarily suspended as a precautionary security measure.

To reinstate your debit card and restore full account access, you must verify your identity through our secure verification portal within the next 12 hours:

hxxps://hdfc-card-reinstate[.]com/verify

During verification, please have your:
- 16-digit Debit Card Number
- Card Expiry Date and CVV
- Internet Banking User ID and Password
- OTP (will be sent to registered mobile)

If verification is not completed within 12 hours, your debit card will be permanently blocked and you will need to visit your nearest HDFC Bank branch to request a replacement card.

Thank you for your patience.

HDFC Bank Digital Security Division
label: phishing
scenario: HDFC Bank debit card suspension phishing requesting full card details, password and OTP
explanation: Phishing email targeting debit card users with a card suspension pretext. The sender domain (hdfc-card-reinstate.com) is not hdfcbank.com. The combination of requesting the full 16-digit card number, CVV, expiry date, NetBanking password, and OTP simultaneously constitutes a complete payment card fraud data harvesting operation. No bank ever requires CVV and online banking credentials together for identity verification. The 12-hour window creates urgency and the branch visit alternative adds false legitimacy. Medium false positive risk due to realistic formatting.

subject: ICICI Bank: Your Fixed Deposit Renewal Instruction Received
body:
Dear Shweta Joshi,

Thank you for your Fixed Deposit renewal instruction.

Renewal Details:
Existing FD Reference: ICICI/FD/2022/MU/48291
Principal Amount: Rs. 2,50,000.00
Interest Earned: Rs. 44,218.00
New FD Principal: Rs. 2,94,218.00 (Cumulative reinvestment)
Renewal Tenor: 2 Years
New Maturity Date: 18 March 2027
Interest Rate: 7.10% p.a.
New FD Reference: ICICI/FD/2025/MU/93847

Your renewed FD will mature on 18 March 2027. Upon maturity, proceeds will be credited to your Savings Account XXXX4821.

To view or modify your FD, log in to ICICI Bank NetBanking or iMobile Pay.

For queries: 1800-1080

Warm regards,
ICICI Bank Fixed Deposits
customercare@icicibank.com | ICICI Bank Tower, BKC, Mumbai 400 051.
label: genuine
scenario: Genuine ICICI Bank Fixed Deposit renewal confirmation with new FD reference number
explanation: Genuine ICICI Bank FD renewal confirmation from customercare@icicibank.com. Contains both the old and new FD reference numbers, exact interest earned, and new maturity date — granular financial data that would require access to the bank's core banking system to generate accurately. No URLs are present; users are simply directed to existing ICICI products (NetBanking or iMobile Pay). The registered ICICI BKC address is accurate. This email is a textbook example of a clean, no-action-required informational bank communication.

subject: FREE PayTm Gold worth Rs.5,000!! Limited Time - Claim in 1 Hour
body:
PAYTM GOLD GIVEAWAY - YOU ARE SELECTED!!

Hi User

Paytm is giving FREE GOLD worth Rs5000 to select customers as part of 10th Anniversary Celebration!!! You have been RANDOMLY SELECTED!!!

CLAIM FREE GOLD:
hxxp://paytm-gold-free[.]in/claim-anniversary

Limited to FIRST 500 customers only!! Only 1 HOUR LEFT to claim!!

You need to:
1. Enter Paytm registered mobile
2. Enter OTP
3. Enter UPI PIN to verify identity
4. Gold will be credited to ur wallet!!

DONT MISS!! Share with friends for BONUS gold!!

Paytm Gold Team
label: phishing
scenario: Paytm Gold prize giveaway phishing harvesting OTP and UPI PIN under anniversary pretext
explanation: Classic high-noise reward bait phishing impersonating Paytm. Domain paytm-gold-free.in is unrelated to paytm.com. The request for a UPI PIN 'to verify identity' is the clearest red flag — UPI PINs authorize fund transfers and are never used for account verification. The 1-hour deadline, 'first 500 customers' scarcity, and 'share with friends' viral distribution instruction are textbook social engineering. Paytm's actual 10th anniversary promotional activities are conducted through the official Paytm app and verified communications, not external domains.

subject: SBI: NEFT Transfer of Rs.50,000 to Ramesh Gupta - Confirmation
body:
Dear Alok Sharma,

Your NEFT transfer has been processed successfully.

Transaction Details:
From Account: XXXX XXXX 8291
Beneficiary Name: Ramesh Gupta
Beneficiary Bank: Axis Bank
Beneficiary Account: XXXX XXXX 4921
IFSC: UTIB0001234
Amount: Rs. 50,000.00
Date & Time: 19 March 2025, 11:24 AM IST
UTR Number: SBIN225031912345
Transaction Reference: YONO/NEFT/2025/0319/AG8821

The transfer will be credited to the beneficiary within 30 minutes as per NEFT batch processing.

If you did not initiate this transaction, please call 1800-11-2211 immediately.

State Bank of India
alerts@sbi.co.in
State Bank Bhavan, Madame Cama Road, Mumbai - 400 021.
label: genuine
scenario: Genuine SBI NEFT outward transfer confirmation with UTR number and beneficiary details
explanation: Genuine SBI NEFT transfer confirmation from alerts@sbi.co.in. Contains a UTR (Unique Transaction Reference) number — the interbank settlement identifier assigned by the NEFT clearing system — which only the originating bank's CBS would generate. Beneficiary details including IFSC are provided, matching real Axis Bank IFSC formatting (UTIB prefix). No URLs present. For funds-out transactions, genuine alerts uniformly mention calling the helpline if unauthorized, with no link to a verification portal. This pattern is a reliable legitimacy signal.

subject: Tech Support: Your HDFC NetBanking has been hacked - Call Us Immediately
body:
URGENT SECURITY ALERT!!!

We have detected that your HDFC Netbanking account has been HACKED by cybercriminals from China!!

Your account balance Rs.2,84,000 is at risk of being stolen in next 30 minutes!!!

CALL OUR HDFC SECURITY HELPLINE IMMEDIATELY:
+91-9XXXXXXXXX (Available 24x7)

Our certified security officer will:
1. Help u freeze ur account remotely
2. Transfer ur funds to a SAFE temporary account
3. Investigate the hack
4. Return ur funds once secure

DO NOT LOG INTO NETBANKING till u call us!!! The hackers will see u!!

HDFC Bank Cyber Cell
support@hdfc-cybersecurity-help[.]in
label: phishing
scenario: HDFC Bank tech support scam email directing victim to call a fake number to 'transfer funds to safety'
explanation: Tech support vishing setup delivered via email — a hybrid phishing/vishing attack. Notably, this email contains no credential-harvesting links; instead, it directs the victim to call a phone number where social engineering will occur in real time. The offer to 'transfer funds to a safe temporary account' is the actual theft mechanism — a common technique in Indian banking tech support scams. 'Hackers from China' is a geopolitical fear amplifier commonly used in Indian-context scams. Telling victims not to log in prevents them from verifying account status through legitimate channels.

subject: ICICI Bank iMobile: New Device Login Successful
body:
Dear Meera Krishnan,

A new device has been added to your ICICI Bank iMobile Pay account.

Device Registration Details:
Device: Samsung Galaxy S24 Ultra
Device ID: SM-S928B-XXXXXXXX
Registration Date: 19 March 2025
Registration Time: 09:41 AM IST
Location: Chennai, Tamil Nadu

If you registered this device, no action is required.

If you did NOT register this device:
1. Call ICICI Bank immediately: 1800-1080
2. Tap 'Deregister Device' in iMobile Pay Settings > Security

For security, iMobile Pay allows a maximum of 3 registered devices at a time.

ICICI Bank
alerts@icicibank.com
label: genuine
scenario: Genuine ICICI iMobile new device registration alert - high FP risk due to security alert format
explanation: Genuine ICICI Bank iMobile device registration alert from alerts@icicibank.com. Contains a specific device model and partial device ID — data only the bank's mobile banking platform would log. Importantly, the response instructions direct users to the bank's phone number and the in-app security settings, not to any URL. High false positive risk stems from the security alert format (new device detected) which superficially resembles phishing security alerts; however, the absence of any link and the in-app remediation instruction are definitive genuine signals.

subject: SBI: Your ATM PIN has been changed - If not done by you, call 1800-11-2211
body:
Dear SBI Customer,

Your SBI ATM/Debit Card PIN for card ending 4821 has been successfully changed.

Date & Time: 19 March 2025, 02:15 PM IST
Card: XXXX XXXX XXXX 4821
Channel: SBI ATM - Branch Code 04921 (Andheri West, Mumbai)

If you did NOT change your ATM PIN, please:
- Block your card immediately by calling: 1800-11-2211 (toll-free, 24x7)
- Or via YONO SBI: Cards > Block/Unblock Card

SBI will never ask for your PIN, OTP, or password.

State Bank of India
alerts@sbi.co.in
label: genuine
scenario: Genuine SBI ATM PIN change confirmation alert with ATM branch location details
explanation: Genuine SBI ATM PIN change notification from alerts@sbi.co.in. The email includes the specific ATM branch code and location where the PIN change occurred — highly specific data only SBI's CBS would log against a transaction. The statement 'SBI will never ask for your PIN, OTP, or password' is a standard SBI security footer. No URLs are present; card blocking is directed to a toll-free number or the YONO app. This pattern — specific transaction location, no links, explicit security reminder — is definitively genuine.

subject: Important: RBI Mandate - Update Your Aadhaar-Bank Linking Before 31 March
body:
Dear Customer,

As per the Reserve Bank of India's latest circular, all bank account holders are required to link their Aadhaar with their bank account by 31st March 2025. Accounts not linked by this date may face restrictions.

We have made this process simple for you. Complete your Aadhaar-Bank linking in 2 minutes:

hxxp://bank-aadhaar-link[.]in/verify

You will need:
- Your Aadhaar Number
- Your Bank Account Number
- OTP from UIDAI-registered mobile

This is a government-mandated requirement. Non-compliance may result in your account being frozen by the Income Tax Department.

Customer Assistance Team
Digital Banking Services
alerts@bank-aadhaar-link[.]in
label: phishing
scenario: RBI/UIDAI Aadhaar-bank linking phishing with fabricated government mandate and IT Department freeze threat
explanation: Phishing leveraging the real but concluded Aadhaar-bank linking drive of 2017-18. The Supreme Court of India's 2018 ruling made Aadhaar-bank linking voluntary, making any mandatory deadline claim fraudulent. The sender has no specific bank identity — the generic 'bank-aadhaar-link.in' domain and 'Customer Assistance Team' signature without bank branding are unusual. Involving the Income Tax Department adds a second layer of authority impersonation. Medium false positive risk because Aadhaar-PAN linking genuinely remains a periodic compliance requirement and users may conflate the two.

subject: HDFC Bank: Your Recurring Deposit of Rs.5,000/month - Installment 8 of 24 Credited
body:
Dear Ananya Desai,

HDFC Bank
alerts@hdfcbank.com
HDFC Bank House, Senapati Bapat Marg, Lower Parel, Mumbai - 400 013.
[Privacy Policy] [Unsubscribe]
label: genuine
scenario: Genuine HDFC Bank Recurring Deposit installment collection confirmation with maturity projection
explanation: Genuine HDFC Bank RD installment confirmation from alerts@hdfcbank.com. The '8 of 24' installment tracking and accumulated balance including interest are detailed accounting outputs only HDFC's core banking system would generate. The estimated maturity amount (Rs.1,28,411) reflects interest calculation — authentic detail a phisher would be unlikely to compute accurately. No URLs are present, and the tone is positive ('Your RD is performing well') rather than alarming. This is a textbook legitimate product notification email.

subject: Exclusive Offer: Upgrade to SBI Platinum Card - Pre-Approved for You
body:
Dear SBI Customer,

Congratulations! You have been pre-approved for an upgrade to the SBI Platinum Debit Card.

Benefits of Upgrading:
- Higher daily transaction limit: Rs.2,00,000
- Airport lounge access (12 complimentary visits/year)
- Enhanced travel insurance up to Rs.50 Lakh
- Cashback on fuel and online shopping

To accept this pre-approved upgrade, please confirm your details:
hxxp://sbi-platinum-upgrade[.]com/accept

You will need to verify your:
- Current Debit Card Number and Expiry
- CVV
- NetBanking Password

This offer expires in 48 hours. Only for eligible SBI account holders.

SBI Cards Division
upgrades@sbi-platinum-upgrade[.]com
label: phishing
scenario: SBI Platinum card upgrade phishing harvesting debit card number, CVV and NetBanking password
explanation: Phishing using a card upgrade lure — a compelling offer targeting users who desire premium banking products. The sender domain (sbi-platinum-upgrade.com) is not sbi.co.in or sbicard.co.in. No legitimate card upgrade process requires customers to submit card CVV and NetBanking password via a link; upgrades are processed through verified in-branch or in-app consent flows. The 48-hour deadline adds urgency to an otherwise attractive offer. Medium false positive risk because pre-approved card offers from banks are genuinely common and may use language similar to this email's opening.

subject: ICICI Bank: Your Annual Account Maintenance Charge of Rs.177 Debited
body:
Dear Farida Shaikh,

Your account has been debited for Annual Maintenance Charge (AMC).

Charge Details:
Account Number: XXXX XXXX 2910
Charge Description: Annual Savings Account Maintenance Fee
Amount Debited: Rs. 177.00 (inclusive of GST @ 18%)
Debit Date: 18 March 2025
Transaction Reference: ICICI/AMC/2025/0318/FS7291

This is a standard annual fee applicable to your Savings Account as per the schedule of charges. You can view the complete schedule at: hxxps://www[.]icicibank[.]com/rates-charges

To waive this charge in future, maintain an Average Monthly Balance (AMB) of Rs. 10,000 or above.

For queries, call 1800-1080 or visit icicibank.com

ICICI Bank Ltd.
customercare@icicibank.com
ICICI Bank Tower, BKC, Mumbai 400 051.
label: genuine
scenario: Genuine ICICI Bank annual account maintenance charge debit notification with GST breakdown
explanation: Genuine ICICI Bank AMC charge notification from customercare@icicibank.com. The GST-inclusive breakdown (Rs.177 at 18% GST) reflects actual Indian banking fee structures — a detail authentic to ICICI's AMC communications. The transaction reference follows ICICI's standard format and the URL points exclusively to icicibank.com's official rates page. The email provides practical advice (maintain Rs.10,000 AMB to waive future charges) which is informational rather than manipulative. All legitimacy signals are present; this is a clean, clearly genuine notification.
